data.employer_name: S. S. CHAVAN & ASSOCIATES CHARTERED ACCOUNTANTS
data.job_description:
Hiring: Paid Assistant / Semi-Qualified CA (CA Inter) – Pune

We are a growing Partnership Firm in Kothrud, Pune, specializing in Accounting, Taxation, and Auditing. We are looking for a dedicated Semi-Qualified CA (CA Inter) to join our team and handle diverse assignments.

Key Responsibilities:
• End-to-end Accounting and Finalization of Accounts.
• Statutory & Internal Audits (including Trust Audits and Tax Audits).
• Compliance management for GST and Income Tax.
• International Taxation (15CA/CB & Equalisation Levy)
• Virtual CFO Services (MIS, Budgeting & Cash Flow)
• Labor Law & ROC Compliances

Candidate Profile:
• Appaeated for CA Inter or CA Final .
• Prior experience in Finalization and Audit at traditonal CA practice Firm is preferred .
• Strong understanding of Tax compliance.

Work-Life & Growth:
• Location: Paud Road, Kothrud, Pune.
• CA Final Leaves: We support your professional growth;
study leaves will be granted based on mutual understanding.

**Job Description: Senior Analyst, Strategic Finance**
• *Role Overview:**
Evolent Health is seeking a Senior Analyst for the Strategic Finance team to support the Product and Technology Business Line. In this role, you will be responsible for supporting the company's financial planning, forecasting, budgeting, and reporting functions. You will collaborate closely with Accounting, Procurement, and department heads to provide insights and financial analysis that drive business decisions. The ideal candidate will have experience in managing monthly accruals, accounting processes, procurement processes, and possess strong analytical and problem-solving abilities to enhance business performance.
• *Key Responsibilities:**
- Work closely with the accounting team to ensure proper documentation and recording of accruals, journal entries, and other financial transactions
- Provide financial oversight of procurement activities and ensure proper accounting treatment of purchasing and vendor transactions
- Maintain and update existing standard models and contribute to the development of new initiatives
- Manage the monthly accrual process to ensure accurate recognition of expenses in accordance with accounting principles and company policies
- Assist with month-end and year-end closing activities to ensure timely and accurate financial reporting
- Support monthly, quarterly, and annual reporting and budgeting cycles with KPIs and insights tailored to Technology and Product leadership
- Maintain, create, automate, and improve Adaptive Insights financial models and reporting templates
- Liaise with other teams under the Finance umbrella to maintain accurate forecasting and partner on month-end variance analyses
- Support special projects, including ROI analysis for new Technology investments, Product launches, and platform enhancements
• *Qualifications Required:**
- Chartered Accountant/CPA, CMA, or Masters degree in Business Finance or equivalent experience
- 3-5 years of experience as an Analyst or Associate in corporate finance (FP&A;), investment banking, or other Finance role with financial modeling experience
- Proactive problem-solving skills with the ability to work independently
- Experience working with Accounts Payable, Accounting, and Procurement teams for invoice, Purchase Order, and accrual related items
- Extensive experience using Microsoft Excel
- Strong analytical abilities with a track record of identifying insights from financial information and translating into business analytics and recommendations with a high level of accuracy

This role will sit within the FP&A Analytics & Digitization team, within the Finance FP&A organization and will be responsible for developing and delivering Financial Data assets aligned with Core ERP systems in service of business needs.Will work to support a C&P, Midstream and Oil & Gas business.Customer & products• Gas & low carbon energy• Production & operations• Innovation & engineering• Regions, cities & solutions• Strategy & sustainability• Trading & Shipping• Communications & advocacy• Finance• Legal• People & cultureAbout the roleThe FP&A Analytics & Digitization Advisor/Manager develops and delivers Financial Data assets aligned with Core ERP systems, supplying to the Mobility, Convenience & Midstream business. It also supports the Global Data Office business collaborators, ensuring regional activities meet global metrics, benchmarks, and reporting standards. The incumbent would be potentially required to work under alternative shift arrangements to ensure appropriate overlap hours with Eastern Standard time or Asia Pacific time zones.Key Roles & Responsibilities• Engagement with a business solution delivery approach: Provide business and technical support, including defect resolution and process training, or bring up issues as needed. Collaborate with the FP&A team to resolve conflicts and understand team duties relative to other interested parties.• Practitioner of Agile methods to deliver solutions: Practice agile methodology to problem identification, project management and solutioning towards self service models.• Uphold collaborator service levels: Implement support tasks according to instructional materials and Service Level Agreements, addressing general support questions from FP&A Product Team and Business teams.• Collaboration: Work with the FP&A Analytics and Digitization Manager to prioritize Product support activities. Engage with FP&A teams to address concerns or questions, providing business and technical support.• Continuous improvement: Identify and implement opportunities for process improvement based on experience and findings.• Data management: support broader safe data culture and implement data governance framework in the business in line with global standards.• Leadership: Lead the MI team in developing new solutions, focusing on datasets, data flows, and visualization. Manage work prioritization within the team. Build capability within the team in data asset delivery and collaborator engagement. Identify and build a future talent pipeline through a clear succession and capability plan.Must have educational qualifications:Business/Finance or Engineering Field Degree level or equivalent or in Data Analytics or related field.Preferred education/certifications:Masters/Bachelor's in computer science/relatedMinimum years of confirmed experience:10 years of relevant post degree experience, working across multiple segmentsPreferred experience:Experience within global, complex and matrix organizations, preferably within an Oil & Gas business or experience in industries such as Retail, Asset-based business, Manufacturing, or Logistics.Must have experiences/skills (To be hired with):• Proficiency in SAP/ERP systems, SQL, Power BI, and data visualization tools.• Experience with Business MI, report building, Data Ownership, Architecture, and Governance processes• Knowledge and experience with Data Visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau)• Hands-on experience in AWS• Skills in various project delivery methodologies, including Agile methodologies' practitioner.• Leadership in driving team performance, forming partnerships, and driving transformation and excellenceYou will be working with a team of finance professionals as part of the Financial Planning and Analysis (FP&A) organization. The FP&A organization is an exciting new team being established by bp to create a center of expertise in the areas of business planning, budgeting and forecasting, financial analysis and economic evaluation. The role will regularly interact and be the main contact point for Business/Functions seniorleadership.In addition to the FP&A team, you will also be partnering with the local finance team and various technical and leadership teams in onsite locations.About bpOur purpose is to deliver energy to the world, today and tomorrow. For over 100 years, bp has focused on discovering, developing, and producing oil and gas in the nations where we operate. We are one of the few companies globally that can provide governments and customers with an integrated energy offering. Delivering our strategy sustainably is fundamental to achieving our ambition to be a net zero company by 2050 or sooner!We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform crucial job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.Additional InformationWe are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.Even though the job is advertised as full time, please contact the hiring manager or the recruiter as flexible working arrangements may be considered.

About KPMG in India

KPMG entities in India are professional services firm(s). These Indian member firms are affiliated with KPMG International Limited. KPMG was established in India in August 1993. Our professionals leverage the global network of firms, and are conversant with local laws, regulations, markets and competition. KPMG has offices across India in Ahmedabad, Bengaluru, Chandigarh, Chennai, Gurugram, Jaipur, Hyderabad, Jaipur, Kochi, Kolkata, Mumbai, Noida, Pune, Vadodara and Vijayawada.

KPMG entities in India offer services to national and international clients in India across sectors. We strive to provide rapid, performance-based, industry-focused and technology-enabled services, which reflect a shared knowledge of global and local industries and our experience of the Indian business environment.

Key Responsibilities
• SA‑CCR Calculations & Implementation
• Perform SA‑CCR exposure calculations for OTC derivatives, SFTs, FX, interest rate products, and cleared trades.
• Review and validate trade‑level attributes: replacement cost, PFE, hedging sets, supervisory factors, netting sets, and collateral impacts.

Model Review & Data Validation
• Ensure alignment with global regulatory requirements: Basel III, Basel IV, BCBS, RBI, FCA, OCC, etc
• Prepare documentation and respond to regulatory/audit queries.
• Risk Analysis & Exposure Monitoring
• Analyze counterparty exposure trends, sensitivity changes, and capital impact under SA‑CCR.
• Work closely with Credit Risk, Market Risk, Treasury, and Front Office on exposure drivers.
• Conduct data quality checks, trade attribute validation, and exception management.
• Partner with Model Validation teams for conceptual soundness, back-testing, and benchmarking.
• Review and enhance methodologies for netting, collateral, and margining.
• Collaborate with Technology, Front Office, Operations, and Regulatory Reporting teams to resolve data gaps and improve exposure computation.
• Participate in global SA‑CCR remediation, transformation, and optimization projects.

Required Skills & Qualifications

Must-Have Skills
• Strong understanding of SA‑CCR, CCR exposure metrics, Basel regulatory frameworks.
• Hands-on exposure to OTC derivatives, SFTs, repo/reverse repo, margin agreements.
• Proficiency in SQL, Python, or other analytical tools.
• Strong quantitative and analytical skills.

Good-to-Have
• Familiarity with IMM, CEM, SIMM, and EAD models.
• Knowledge of collateral management, CSA terms, and netting agreements.
• Understanding of capital planning and regulatory reporting.
• Certifications: FRM, CFA, CQF, or equivalent.

Equal employment opportunity information

KPMG India has a policy of providing equal opportunity for all applicants and employees regardless of their color, caste, religion, age, sex/gender, national origin, citizenship, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability or other legally protected status. KPMG India values diversity and we request you to submit the details below to support us in our endeavor for diversity. Providing the below information is voluntary and refusal to submit such information will not be prejudicial to you.

data.employer_name: Milind Sangoram and Company, Chartered Accountants
data.job_description:
Milind Sangoram and Company, Chartered Accountants is a dynamic and innovative firm committed to providing exceptional financial services to our clients. We specialize in corporate taxation and audit, and our team is dedicated to delivering tailored solutions to meet our clients' diverse needs. As part of our commitment to nurturing talent, we are excited to announce opportunities in the area of Tax and Audit..

Role Description

This is a full-time on-site Audit and Tax Manager role located in Pune. The person will be responsible for assisting the Chartered Accountants in preparing financial statements, bookkeeping, auditing, and taxation. The candidate will also have to collate and analyze data from various sources, create reports, and assist in formulating business strategies.

Qualifications
• Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field with 3 years of experience in Accounting or has completed their CA articleship.
• Good working knowledge of accounting principles and standards
• Excellent numerical and analytical skills
• Strong communication and interpersonal skills
• Ability to work under pressure and meet deadlines
• Proficient in MS Office and accounting software
• Interest in the field of finance and taxation

Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title and Summary

Senior Accountant

Overview:
• Are you motivated to work in a team that provides global services?
• Do you have lease accounting experience that you’re looking to leverage in a new role at a great organization?
• Do you like to simplify, standardize, and transform existing processes?
If so, we have an exciting, Sr. Accountant opportunity for you on the Lease Accounting team.

Role:
Sr. Accountant - Lease Accounting Team
• Maintain accounting for leased assets and related occupancy expenses.
• Provide guidance and support to the regions and business units
• Meet, or exceed established departmental targets as they related to various KPI’s.
• Drive excellent customer service for applicable stakeholders
• Seek automation and efficiency improvements in all processes and measure progress
• Partner with the Financial Planning and Analysis team to aid in budgeting and forecasting as it relates to occupancy expenses
• Perform and/or review account reconciliations
• Miscellaneous project work, as required
• Support SOX control compliance
• Assist in onboarding M&A entities, as needed
• Other duties as required

All About You:
Education/Certification
• Bachelor of Science degree in Accounting or Finance
• CPA or Chartered Accountant, preferred

Knowledge/Experience/Skills
• Excellent technical knowledge of US GAAP and current accounting concepts (specifically within lease accounting under the ASC 842 standard)
• Experience identifying and implementing process improvement activities
• Progressive accounting experience
• Previous work in a large global environment
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
• Strong verbal and written communication skills
• Strong customer service and business acumen
• Strong sense of urgency and ability to prioritize under tight deadlines

Corporate Security Responsibility

All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:
• Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;
• Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;
• Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and
• Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

Job Details

About the job

Our team members are at the heart of everything we do. At Cencora, we are united in our responsibility to create healthier futures, and every person here is essential to us being able to deliver on that purpose. If you want to make a difference at the center of health, come join our innovative company and help us improve the lives of people and animals everywhere. Apply today!

Job Summary

We are seeking a detail-oriented and experienced Finance & Accounting professional to manage end-to-end accounting, financial reporting, statutory compliance, and internal controls. The role is critical to ensuring accurate financial statements, regulatory compliance, effective cash flow management, and timely statutory and vendor payments.

Key Responsibilities

• Own monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements and close process

• Lead statutory, tax, and internal audits; ensure a strong control environment and full compliance with SOX and Internal controls over financial reporting.

• Manage direct and indirect tax compliance (TDS, GST, advance tax, corporate tax), working closely with internal team as well as advisors

• Oversee payroll accounting and statutory compliance (PF, ESI, NPS)

• Ensure strong balance-sheet controls, reconciliations, and cash flow management

• Manage fixed asset accounting, capitalization, and physical verification

• Oversee accounts payable, vendor reconciliations, statutory/vendor payments and travel & reimbursements

• Ensure compliance with RBI, MCA, FEMA, labor laws and other regulatory compliances and proactively engage with relevant stakeholders on upcoming changes

• Drive process improvements, controls, and reporting quality
• Serve as the primary Accounting and Finance point of contact (POC) for senior leadership, collaborating closely with Directors and HR Partners to provide oversight, guidance, and strategic support for departmental initiatives.
• Supports Intercompany(IC) billing and collection and ensuring elimination with IC by maintaining appropriate reconciliations.

Qualifications & Experience
• Chartered Accountant (CA) certification required.
• 12–15 years of experience in financial accounting and controllership with with at least 3+ years in a senior controllership or finance leadership role.
• Strong knowledge of Indian tax, regulatory, and compliance requirements
• Experience with ERP systems and internal controls
• Proven experience managing shared services or global capability centers supporting multinational operations is strongly preferred
• Public accounting experience at a Big 4 firm is strongly preferred
• Stakeholder Influence: Necessary for engaging with local/global leadership, local auditors/advisor, and regulatory bodies.
• Excellent verbal and written communication skills; ability to present findings to executive leadership.
• This role has potential to grow to a regional controller role of South-Asia, so multi region experience is an advantage.

Schedule

Full time

Affiliated Companies
Affiliated Companies: CENCORA BUSINESS SERVICES INDIA PRIVATE LIMITED

Equal Employment Opportunity

Cencora is committed to providing equal employment opportunity without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, genetic information, national origin, age, disability, veteran status or membership in any other class protected by federal, state or local law.

The company’s continued success depends on the full and effective utilization of qualified individuals. Therefore, harassment is prohibited and all matters related to recruiting, training, compensation, benefits, promotions and transfers comply with equal opportunity principles and are non-discriminatory.

Cencora is committed to providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities during the employment process which are consistent with legal requirements. If you wish to request an accommodation while seeking employment, please call 888.692.2272 or email hrsc@cencora.com. We will make accommodation determinations on a request-by-request basis. Messages and emails regarding anything other than accommodations requests will not be returned

Company Description

Alburj Engineering Consultants is a leading multidisciplinary engineering consultancy firm established in 1982 with a strong track record across the UAE, Egypt and KSA. During more than 40 Years we maintained an attractive work environment that maintains work-life balance combining technical excellence with integrity and collaboration to support our impactful infrastructure and built environments.

We are expanding operations and seeking a Senior Accountant to support our finance operations in Pune, India.

Role Description

We are seeking an experienced Senior Accountant to join our growing organization based in Pune, Maharashtra, India to be responsible to Handle India Office accounting operations and maintaining accurate books, statutory compliance, and financial reporting, end-to-end accounting, ensuring compliance with all Indian laws, Companies Acts and regulatory filings, coordinating with external Auditors and tax Consultants.

The ideal candidate is hands-on, detail-oriented, and comfortable operating in a scaling, multinational environment with both local autonomy and global collaboration.

Key Responsibilities
• Manage end-to-end accounting and monthly closing.
• Coordinate with External Auditors, Tax Consultants, Banks and Governmental Authorities.
• Maintain accurate financial records and statutory books
• Ensure timely communication and provide Operational and Financial Reporting Support for UAE branch.
• Support consolidation and global reporting needs.
• Work closely with HR, Operations, and Global Finance teams.
• Ensure compliance with all applicable Indian Laws and regulations ( Direct/indirect Tax, Companies Act, External and Tax Audits,………).

Experience & Qualifications
• Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or related field
• 5–10 years of relevant accounting experience in India.
• CA, CMA, ACCA, or CPA is preferred
• Strong understanding of Indian statutory compliance & reporting.
• Practical experience with accounting / ERP systems such as ( Tally, QuickBooks, ERP Next,……… )
• Excellent in Microsoft Office skills Mainly Excel.
• Good Command in English Language.

What We’re Looking For
• High professional ethics and integrity
• Comfortable working within structured internal policies
• Detail-oriented and collaborative in a growing multinational environment
• Live in Pune, Maharashtra, India or willing to reallocate.
• Experience working with multinational or UAE-based companies is preferred.
• Prior experience in a startup, or Back Office Companies is a plus.

If you’re ready to progress your career with a respected global engineering consultancy, please apply by sharing your updated CV.

data.job_title: Lead Technology Risk Management Analyst(RBI,Internal & External Audit)
data.job_description:
• *Overview**

AI & Decision Product Enablement Program (AI & DPE) is an internal product suite that enables intelligent decisions for market facing products. This program provides sophisticated, industry-leading intelligence with a range of capabilities that structure and apply complex business logic across the payment journey and beyond to inform and accelerate decisions at scale. This is achieved by leveraging supercomputing capabilities, sophisticated business rules, AI technologies, a streaming big data cluster, high speed in-memory data caching, APIs, & UIs to enrich data and provide real-time decisions.The AI DPE Risk Management team is looking for a Lead Technology Risk Analyst to drive our compliance and regulatory frameworks in India. The ideal candidate is passionate about proactive readiness to control testing, a willingness to learn complex environments, and has experience in the auditing and regulatory space, particularly in India. This is a high impact role for a risk minded, detail oriented leader who thrives at the intersection of technology, regulation, and operational excellence.

The Lead Technology Risk Analyst will:
• Provide control, compliance, and regulatory guidance to developers, architects, and project managers.
• Interpret and operationalize new and evolving regulatory requirements, including RBI (PSS, IDL), DPDP Act, privacy, and broader data regulations.
• Assist in the design and implementation of preventative and detective controls within the first-line environment.
• Partner closely with US-based counterparts to manage and continuously mature the program's global control landscape.
• *All About You**
• 8-12 years of overall career experience into managing audits and technology risk management
• Has had audit and regulatory experience (specifically RBI (PSS & IDL), and has conducted internal and external audits
• You have managed large audits end to end with RBI
• You have been a part of internal and external audits managing various products and teams on a large scale
• Passionate about building proactive readiness frameworks and strengthening first line control environments
• Able to translate regulatory requirements into actionable control practices within AI driven and data intensive environments
• Adept at integrating IT risk program frameworks with enterprise risk functions, designing IT risk metrics, risk aggregation and reporting concepts
• Ability to communicate effectively with cross-functional Data Science, Development teams, regulatory agents, and the core product business teams
• Thrives working with highly technical products in a fast-paced delivery environment
• Possession of at least one relevant professional certification, such as (but not limited to) CIA, CISA, CISM, or CFE.

In this position, you will:
• Partner with leadership to identify control gaps and drive resolution, solutioning, and long-term risk reduction.
• Act as liason between our first line engineers and architects and external audit
• Collaborate with internal risk, compliance, and governance teams to manage, monitor, and maintain regulatory and compliance controls across the program.
• Facilitate scope and impact assessment to identify control and regulatory requirement applicability
• Support program and product-level audits, and develop readiness materials for enterprise or external audit engagements.

Stock Analyst

Experience: 4 to 5 Years

Must Have certificate NISM 21B.

Summary A Stock Analyst is responsible for analyzing financial data, market trends, and company performance to provide investment recommendations on stocks. The role involves conducting in-depth research, evaluating financial statements, and identifying investment opportunities to support portfolio management or client advisory.

Key Responsibilities
• Conduct fundamental and technical analysis of stocks and sectors.
• Analyze financial statements, annual reports, and company performance.
• Track market trends, economic indicators, and industry developments.
• Prepare research reports and investment recommendations (Buy/Sell/Hold).
• Monitor portfolio performance and stock movements.
• Build and maintain financial models and valuation models.
• Present research insights to portfolio managers, clients, or management.
• Stay updated with regulatory changes and market news.

Required Skills
• Strong knowledge of equity markets and stock valuation
• Financial modeling and data analysis
• Understanding of fundamental and technical analysis
• Analytical and research skills
• Advanced Excel and financial tools knowledge
• Good communication and presentation skills

Qualification
• Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, Commerce, or Business

Must Have certificate NISM 21B.

Ashok Chhajed & Associates is 40 years old Firm having Head Office at Ahmedabad and branches on PAN India basis which is having Assignments of all kind of industries.

The Firm is recruiting a Chartered Accountant having Post qualification experience of 7 to 10 years in the field of Account, Audit, Income Tax and GST who will be deployed in PSU at Pune on behalf of us.

The applicant not having knowledge of INDAs and SAP should not apply the same

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: ₹125,000.00 - ₹175,000.00 per month

Experience:
• Audit and Accounts along with Taxation: 7 years (Preferred)

License/Certification:
• Chartered Accountant (Preferred)

Work Location: In person

Summary of the role-
Conducts Financial and Corporate risk-based audits and advisory reviews, supports business process SOX and financial process SOX. Leads the planning, execution, reporting and follow-up of audit engagements. Develops, monitors and assists in executing the testing strategy for audit engagements.

What part will you play?
• Support the execution of all aspects of the audit process, including planning, client coordination, fieldwork, data analysis, work paper documentation, reporting, and remediation validation, with direction from senior team members
• Strong focus on understanding the company’s operational processes and supporting technology components to perform integrated, risk-based audits. Evaluations include assessing the design and effectiveness of manual and automated controls that support the company’s business processes.
• Document a thorough understanding of business process steps including interrelationships between departments to identify potential risks, recommend process improvement opportunities, and evaluate internal control execution.
• Evaluate root cause factors for audit testing exceptions and recommend practical solutions that reduce risk and strengthen business processes and controls.
• Ensure audit testing work papers are documented in a consistent and high-quality manner while executing project tasks in adherence to established timelines.
• Build and develop Internal Audit’s brand within the company through meaningful relationship building.
• Support the coordination of audit activities with management, co-source providers and external auditors.
• Enable continuous improvement of the Internal Audit department by identifying and communicating enhancement opportunities to department leadership.
What are the qualifications and experience for this role?
• 2-6 years of experience in internal audit/ Sarbanes-Oxley Act (SOX) control testing.
• Experienced candidate from Big 4 auditing firm or payment processing company is preferred
• Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Auditing, Business Management or a related field.
• Candidate with CA qualification or CIA, CFE certification is preferred
What are the desired skills and capabilities?
• Demonstrates an understanding of the financial aspects of business operations, including:
• Accounts Payable
• Accounts Receivable
• Deferred Assets
• Equity
• Financial Reporting and Close
• Fixed Assets
• Forecasting and Budgeting
• Human Resources and payroll
• Pricing and Revenue Recognition
• Revenue and Settlement Processing
• Sales and Partner Commissions
• Software Capitalization
• Ability to work in a complex and evolving environment.
• Strong project management and execution skills, including prioritizing tasks, balancing workload, anticipating next steps, and adapting to change.
• Strong communication and presentation skills with an ability to tailor communications to different audiences.
• Prepare clear, concise, and accurate audit documentation.
• Pursue work with enthusiasm, energy, drive, and team collaboration.
• Maintain a positive, professional, and team-oriented attitude.
• Establish and build effective relationships.
• Familiarity with general information technology control practices related to information security, technology infrastructure management, the systems development lifecycle, program change management, user access controls, systems architecture, and business continuity and disaster recovery.
• SOX/SSAE16 control testing

data.job_title: Fund Services.PE Senior Fund Accountant | Pune, IN
data.job_description:
Fund Services.PE Senior Fund Accountant

Job Description

Our Benefits

Your wellbeing is of paramount importance to us, and central to our success. We provide a range of benefits, training and education support, and flexible working arrangements to help you achieve success in your career while balancing personal needs. Ask us about specific benefits in your location.

We embrace diversity, prioritizing the hiring of people from diverse backgrounds. Our inclusive culture is a source of pride and strength, fostering innovation and mutual respect.

Citco welcomes and encourages applications from people with disabilities. Accommodations are available upon request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection.

Responsibilities

Your Role:

• Maintaining books and records and calculating fund Net Asset Values;
• Reviewing and/or preparing financial statements and associated reports, including performance metrics;
• Reviewing and/or calculating carried interest/performance/incentive fees;
• Reviewing and/or preparing calculations and investor allocations for capital calls, distributions and equalisations;
• Coordinating and/or processing payments and other transactions;
• Acting as a primary day-to-day contact with clients and other fund participants;
• Work closely with the Investor Relations team to respond to investor queries and to disseminate capital activity and other investor notices;
• Participating in projects that may be defined from time to time in connection with the continuing expansion and evolution of the business;
• Identifying and escalating any material issues effecting service delivery;
• Any other functions required as part of the business of the Company.
Qualifications

About You:

• Bachelor's degree in Accounting
• Professional accounting qualification - CA, CPA, ACCA or similar
• Minimum of 2 - 4 years' experience in accounting for the financial services sector, particularly Private Equity, Asset Management or Fund Administration.
• Working knowledge of Investran highly desirable (or other Private Equity system e.g. E-Front)
• Experience in Limited Partnership accounting
• Private equity/real estate fund administration experience
• Experienced in reviewing and preparing general ledger and financial statements.
• Proficiency in English (both verbal and written).
• Motivated, bright and diligent individual who is driven to meet deadlines.
• A team player who is able to manage individual responsibilities.
• Sound judgment, problem solving and analytical skills.
• Proficient with Microsoft Applications (Excel, Word, PowerPoint etc.).

About the Role-

Seeking a qualified fresher Chartered Accountant or a Semi Qualified CA with a strong background in Internal Audit to join our team in Pune. The ideal candidate will have significant experience in auditing listed companies, particularly in the Manufacturing and IT sectors. This role involves leading audit engagements from planning through reporting, ensuring compliance, evaluating internal controls, and driving value addition for clients.

Key Responsibilities:
• Plan, execute, and manage end-to-end internal audits, including audit committee presentations.
• Evaluate internal controls, ensure regulatory compliance, and review the accuracy of financial records.
• Prepare detailed audit reports, maintain working papers, and present findings to senior management.
• Recommend improvements to systems, processes, and control frameworks.
• Ensure adherence to the ICAI's Code of Ethics and firm's values.
• Stay current with auditing standards, regulatory changes, and conduct internal trainings/seminars.
• Collaborate effectively with clients and internal teams; build and maintain strong professional relationships.
• Identify and communicate new opportunities or critical issues to reporting managers or partners.
• Support LLP-level initiatives and be willing to stretch beyond working hours when required.

Qualifications & Skills:
• Fresher Chartered Accountant (CA) or Semi Qualified CA
• Articleship must be majorly in Internal Audit
• Experience with listed companies, preferably in Manufacturing and IT sectors
• Strong knowledge of internal audit, risk management, and compliance frameworks
• Familiarity with audit tools and advanced MS Excel skills
• Excellent analytical, documentation, and reporting skills
• Strong communication and presentation abilities
• Ability to work independently and in a team environment

Key Benefits:
• Opportunity to work with reputed listed clients across sectors
• Strong learning curve and professional development
• Supportive work environment with emphasis on quality and ethics
• Five-day work week ensuring healthy work-life balance

NIQ may utilize artificial intelligence (AI) tools at various stages of the recruitment process, including résumé screening, candidate assessments, interview scheduling, job matching, communication support, and certain administrative tasks that help streamline workflows. These tools are intended to improve efficiency and support fair and consistent evaluation based on job-related criteria. All use of AI is governed by NIQ’s principles of fairness, transparency, human oversight, and inclusion. Final hiring decisions are made exclusively by humans. NIQ regularly reviews its AI tools to help mitigate bias and ensure compliance with applicable laws and regulations. If you have questions, require accommodations, or wish to request human review were permitted by law, please contact your local HR representative. For more information, please visit NIQ’s AI Safety Policies and Guiding Principles: https://www.nielseniq.com/global/en/ai-safety-policies.

NIQ is the world’s leading consumer intelligence company, delivering the most complete understanding of consumer buying behavior and revealing new pathways to growth. In 2023, NIQ combined with GfK, bringing together the two industry leaders with unparalleled global reach. With a holistic retail read and the most comprehensive consumer insights—delivered with advanced analytics through state-of-the-art platforms—NIQ delivers the Full View™. All employment decisions at NIQ are made without regard to race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy, sexual orientation, or gender identity), national origin, age, disability, genetic information, marital status, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws. Role Description Statement

The Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A) professional is responsible for driving financial planning, budgeting, forecasting, and performance analysis to support strategic decision-making in close alignment with the local controllers. Leveraging advanced data analytics and insights, the FP&A Controller role ensures the accuracy and integrity of financial information.

Key Responsibilities (High Level)

• Help in conducting profitability and performance reporting, providing essential data for decision-making

• Support collections reporting and analytics, ensuring accurate and timely information is available for decision-making

• Help oversee period end close processes, generating accurate and insightful financial reports and analytics

• Help oversee CAPEX planning, forecasting, investment strategy, period-end close activities, and reporting
• Support the adherence to International Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) in all financial reporting and processes. Ensure internal controls are in place for the processes performed by this role
• Comply with relevant QESH regulations

Education and experience

• Minimum of 3-5 years of experience in financial planning and analysis or related finance roles

Critical success factors

About the Role:

Zywave, Inc., a leading Insurtech SaaS provider is looking to add a Pune, India based Senior Accountant to its global corporate accounting team. The Senior Accountant is responsible for executing and improving general ledger accounting, the month-end close processes, account reconciliations, and financial analysis across multiple entities and currencies. This role ensures compliance with US GAAP, supports tax and audit activities, and drives process improvements, including automation initiates using AI-powered tools.

Key Responsibilities
• Manage and execute timely and accurate month-end and year-end close tasks, including journal entries, account reconciliations and variance analysis.
• Serve as the subject matter expert in technical accounting areas, including but not limited to Commissions, Leases, Fixed Assets, Prepaids, and Intercompany.
• Work closely with Finance, Operations, Legal, HR, and Sales to ensure accurate financial reporting.
• Identify and implement process improvements, including automation using AI-powered tools and bots.
• Ensure compliance with internal controls and statutory accounting guidance.

Essential Tasks:

Commissions Accounting: Partner with HR and Sales Operations to understand commission plans and ensure proper accounting treatment. Manage capitalized commission accounting, including identifying eligible costs, calculating amortization schedules, and maintaining supporting schedules and documentation. Record monthly commissions earned expense and reconcile related balance sheet accounts.

Lease Accounting: Manage end to end lease accounting in accordance with ASC 842, including classification, initial recognition, and ongoing measurement. Prepare monthly lease journal entries, reconciliations, and supporting documentation.

Fixed Assets: Oversee the fixed asset lifecycle, including capitalization, disposals, transfers, and depreciation. Maintain the fixed asset register and perform monthly reconciliations of fixed asset accounts and depreciation expense.

Prepaid Expenses: Manage the prepaid expense schedule and ensure timely amortization of balances. Review vendor invoices to determine proper prepaid treatment and coding. Prepare monthly journal entries and reconciliations for all prepaid accounts. Collaborate with department owners to validate timing and accuracy of prepaid allocations.

Other Tasks: Perform other assigned tasks which may include expense accruals, intercompany variance reconciliations, cash transactions, and other continuous improvement and strategic projects

Qualifications:
• Bachelor of Commerce (B. Com), specifically in Accounting & Finance (BAF) or USA bachelor’s degree in accounting.
• Indian Chartered Accountancy (CA) or USA Certified Public Accountant (CPA) preferred.
• Strong understanding of US GAAP.
• 3+ years of professional corporate accounting experience.
• Experience with month-end close including journal entries preparation, account reconciliations, and analysis.
• Excellent analytical, communication, and problem-solving skills.
• Advanced Microsoft Excel skills (e.g. pivot tables, VLOOKUP, macros).

Proficiency with the following systems is a plus:
• Sage Intacct
• Microsoft Office & Related Tools (e.g. Excel, Word, PowerPoint, Teams, Outlook, Power BI)
• Experience with AI and automation tools (e.g. Microsoft Copilot)

Why Zywave?

Zywave is a United States based company with operations in Pune India. Zywave empowers insurers and brokers to drive profitable growth and thrive in today’s escalating risk landscape. Only Zywave delivers a powerful Performance Multiplier, bringing together transformative, ecosystem-wide capabilities to amplify impact across data, processes, people, and customer experiences. More than 15,000 insurers, MGAs, agencies, and brokerages trust Zywave to sharpen risk assessment, strengthen client relationships, and enhance operations. Additional information can be found at www.zywave.com.

How to Apply

If you meet the above qualifications and are excited about the opportunity, please email your updated resume to Anindita.Acharjee@zywave.com with your availability and salary expectations. We will connect with you shortly regarding your fitment for the role.

As a Day Trader at Exvix Quantitative Research Pvt Ltd, you will be responsible for executing trades within the financial markets daily. This role requires a keen understanding of market trends, risk management strategies, and the ability to make quick, informed decisions in a fast-paced environment.

Responsibilities:

• Analyze market data and economic indicators to identify trading opportunities.
• Develop and implement trading strategies based on technical and fundamental analysis.
• Manage risk effectively by setting stop-loss orders and adhering to proper risk management protocols.

Career advancement opportunities within our growing organization.

At Ecolab, you can help take on some of the world’s most meaningful challenges—delivering critical insights and innovative solutions that enable our customers to achieve clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments. With our global reach and ambitious growth plans, you will have the opportunity to shape your future and make a lasting impact. Are you ready to make a difference?

Job Description:

Ecolab is seeking an FP&A Director responsible for leading financial planning and analysis activities across the organization. This role serves as a key point of contact for Division Finance Managers and enables them to drive best‑in‑class financial performance and business insights. The position requires strong partnerships across multiple stakeholders and offers significant exposure and growth opportunities. As FP&A Director, you will work closely with divisional finance teams to drive results and strengthen financial rigor.

What’s in it for You?
• Join a fast-growing organization offering a competitive salary and benefits.
• Influence and shape your career with a company committed to continuous growth.
• Work in an environment that values diverse people, perspectives, and ideas.
• Feel proud to contribute to a company that ensures clean water, safe food, abundant energy, and healthy environments.

What You Will Do
• Lead a team of division FP&A managers in GBS+ and ensure timely completion of divisional analytics packs with robust insights explaining business performance drivers.
• Ensure delivery of services in alignment with established service-level agreements (SLAs) with divisions.
• Partner with Process Leads to drive divisional FP&A transformation initiatives.
• Collaborate closely with division finance leaders to help FP&A managers understand internal and external business dynamics and their impact on financial outcomes.
• Lead and coordinate the development of annual plans, ensuring strong collaboration among all key stakeholders.
• Oversee monthly close and forecast processes—including providing direction, reviewing results, supporting system loads, and preparing content for senior leadership business reviews.
• Ensure teams deliver accurate, sustainable content for monthly business reviews (MBRs) and provide ad hoc financial analysis for decision‑making.
• Collaborate with broader finance and business teams to support critical reporting needs and enhance reporting capabilities and tools.

Qualifications
• Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, or Economics
• CA or equivalent professional qualification preferred
• Minimum 18 years of finance experience, with strong exposure to FP&A
• Experience in an FMCG environment strongly preferred
• Proficiency in BI tools such as Tableau or Power BI
• Strong understanding of financial and commercial reporting fundamentals
• Exceptional analytical, problem‑solving, and decision‑making skills
• Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence stakeholders at all levels
• Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast‑paced environment

Our Commitment to a Culture of Inclusion & Belonging
Ecolab is committed to fair and equal treatment of associates and applicants and furthering the principles of Equal Opportunity to Employment. We will recruit, hire, promote, transfer and provide opportunities for advancement based on individual qualifications and job performance in all matters affecting employment, compensation, benefits, working conditions, and opportunities for advancement. Ecolab will not discriminate against any associate or applicant for employment because of race, religion, color, creed, national origin,citizenship status, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity and expressions, genetic information, marital status, age, or disability.

• Be part of a European MNC.
• Work directly with the leadership.

About Our Client

This opportunity is with a large organisation within the Energy sector, specialising in Renewables. The company is known for its robust operations and commitment to excellence in the energy domain.

Job Description
• Lead financial structuring, valuation, and negotiation of M&A and partnership transactions.
• Evaluate commercial assumptions, identify key value drivers, and assess downside risks.
• Design shareholder protections and governance frameworks to safeguard investment value.
• Analyse impact of transactions on credit metrics, leverage, and capital structure.
• Support covenant monitoring and ensure compliance with lender obligations.

The Successful Applicant

A successful Senior Manager Corporate Finance should have:
• A strong educational background in finance, accounting, or a related field.
• Proven experience in end-to-end M&A, corporate finance within the Energy, Infrastructure or Industrials sector.
• At least 6-8 years of relevant experience post qualification.
• Expertise M&A deal structuring.
• Knowledge of financial regulations and compliance standards.
• Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities.

What's on Offer
• Opportunity to work with a large organisation in the Energy sector.
• A collaborative and professional company culture in Pune.
• Challenging and rewarding career growth opportunities within the industry.

data.job_title: Lead Analyst - FP&A and Power BI
data.job_description:
Responsibilities

Lead Analyst - Accounting

Exp - 6 - 10 years

Shift Timings - 1.30 PM - 10.30 PM
• Build, maintain, and enhance Power BI dashboards to support forecasting, performance tracking, and management reporting for SCBUs.
• Design intuitive, leadership‑friendly visualizations that highlight KPIs, financial trends, and business drivers.
• Automate manual reports and recurring processes using Power BI, Power Query, and other analytical tools to improve efficiency and reduce turnaround time.
• Collect, clean, aggregate, and validate data from multiple SCBUs and enterprise systems to ensure accuracy and reliability of reporting.
• Establish and maintain data links between different sources, building data models, and maintaining reporting datasets to support end‑to‑end dashboard operations. (Power BI datasets, Oracle Essbase/ Peoplesoft, Advanced Excel, PowerPoint, etc.) to create a seamless reporting ecosystem.
• Support forecasting, actuals reporting, and value analysis by preparing data inputs and performing first‑level variance reviews.
• Provide initial analytical support for performance metrics, and operational KPIs, highlighting anomalies and key insights.
• Assist in documenting processes, data flows, and dashboard refresh cycles.
• Highlight trends, outliers, and data inconsistencies.
• Support the SCBU FP&A team in process redesign, reporting improvements, and system enhancements to align with evolving business requirements.
• Work collaboratively with SCBU FP&A teams and cross‑BU finance partners to share best practices and ensure alignment of data and reporting standards.
• Gain a strong understanding of financial data structures, systems, and reporting tools used within the organization.
• Manage assigned deliverables independently, including prioritizing multiple tasks, meeting deadlines, and maintaining high‑quality outputs.
• Prepare clear, visually effective presentations and insights for leadership consumption.

Qualifications

Education & Experience
• Master’s degree / Professional Degree / Relevant Industry Certification
• Preferably Chartered Accountant or MBA Finance
• 6-10 years’ of relevant experience in handling complex datasets, analytics, reporting, automation & PowerBI skills. (preferably in BFSI Industry, either in GCC / service provider environment)

Technical Skills
• Strong hands‑on experience with Power BI—data modeling, DAX, dashboards, and report publishing.
• Proficiency in Oracle Essbase/ Peoplesoft, Excel (advanced formulas, pivot tables, Power Query), PowerPoint.
• Comfort working with large datasets and connecting to varied data sources.
• Basic understanding of financial statements (Income Statement, Balance Sheet).
• Experience with data cleansing, validation, and transformation.
• Exposure to SQL or other database/querying tools

Analytical & Soft Skills
• Strong problem‑solving ability and high attention to detail.
• Good communication skills with ability to translate data into insights.
• Ability to work independently, manage multiple tasks, and prioritize effectively.
• Eagerness to learn and develop deeper financial acumen.

About the Role:

The Supply Chain Risk Analyst will be responsible for identifying, assessing, and managing risks associated with third parties. The analyst will work closely with various departments and stakeholders to develop and implement effective risk management strategies. Additionally, the analyst will be responsible for understanding and mitigating Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) risks within the supply chain. This includes evaluating the environmental impact of suppliers, ensuring fair labor practices, and maintaining compliance with governance standards.

Responsibilities:
• Risk Assessment: Assess risks including Geographical, Reputational, Cyber Security, Sanctions, Human Resources, Business Continuity, Anti-Bribery/Anti-Corruption, Artificial Intelligence, ESG, and Privacy.
• Daily Operations: Perform day-to-day responsibilities of the SCRM program, including intake and handling of requests for new third parties, gathering documentation for due diligence and onboarding tasks, and maintaining the SCRM database.
• Monitoring: Monitor third parties for changes in their risk profile, including mergers and acquisitions, regulatory changes, and other external factors.
• Risk Assessment: Conduct risk assessments on new and existing third parties.
• Database Maintenance: Ensure the integrity of the SCRM database by accurately documenting and consistently updating contact information, products, and services to maintain reliable and trustworthy data.
• Reporting: Assist in generating standardized reports to inform business owners and management on third-party risk and performance.
• Risk Management: Encourage and enable effective risk management change.
• Support: Provide support and expertise to business owners on third-party relationship management.
• Incident Management: Conduct ongoing management of escalations and incidents related to due diligence and monitoring of third parties.
• Remediation: Facilitate or assist in the remediation of any third-party-related findings or issues.
• Program Improvement: Contribute to the improvement of the Supply Chain Risk Management Program.
• Vendor Communication: Communicate with third-party vendors to address identified risks and ensure compliance with contractual obligations and regulatory requirements.
• Industry Trends: Stay abreast of industry trends and best practices related to Supply Chain Risk Management.
• Special Projects: Assist with any special projects as needed.
• Bachelor’s degree in Information Security, Business Administration, or a related field.
• Minimum of 2 years of experience in third-party risk

Our Interview Practices

To maintain a fair and genuine hiring process, we kindly ask that all candidates participate in interviews without the assistance of AI tools or external prompts. Our interview process is designed to assess your individual skills, experiences, and communication style. We value authenticity and want to ensure we’re getting to know you—not a digital assistant. To help maintain this integrity, we ask to remove virtual backgrounds and include in-person interviews in our hiring process. Please note that use of AI-generated responses or third-party support during interviews will be grounds for disqualification from the recruitment process.

Applicants may be required to appear onsite at a Wolters Kluwer office as part of the recruitment process.

data.job_title: Analyst Senior, Business Analysis (capital markets, securities processing)
data.job_description:
Position Type

Full time

Type Of Hire

Experienced (relevant combo of work and education)

Education Desired

Bachelor's Degree

Job Description

As the world works and lives faster, FIS is leading the way. Our fintech solutions touch nearly every market, company and person on the planet. Our teams are inclusive and diverse. Our colleagues work together and celebrate together. If you want to advance the world of fintech, we’d like to ask you~ Are you FIS?

About The Role

The Business Analyst will work as a liaison between the product management and development team to analyze the business requirements, convert those into functional specifications and get projects ready for development. The candidate will also need to participate in integration testing and validating new functions to certify they meet business requirements. The role also involves working with the SQA team & the Release Engineering team to make sure projects are released to clients on time. The candidate needs to possess strong analytical skills, excellent communication abilities, and a deep understanding of business operations and the overall SDLC processes.

What You Will Be Doing
• Analyzing Business requirement
• Preparing Functional Specifications documents
• Functional/Integration testing
• Creating and maintaining project artifacts
• Project Implementation in co-ordination with SQA and Release Engineering

What You Bring
• Understanding of trading and settlement of Equities, Options and Fixed Income securities
• Knowledge of Order Management Systems & awareness on Back/Middle office functions
• Understanding of risk monitoring and margining methods
• Knowhow on options & equity strategy parings
• Knowledge of Agile/Scrum practices
• Knowhow of SDLC processes

What We Offer You

Prior experience as a Business Analyst involved in the US trading/margining systems is preferred.

Privacy Statement

FIS is committed to protecting the privacy and security of all personal information that we process in order to provide services to our clients. For specific information on how FIS protects personal information online, please see the Online Privacy Notice.

Sourcing Model

Recruitment at FIS works primarily on a direct sourcing model; a relatively small portion of our hiring is through recruitment agencies. FIS does not accept resumes from recruitment agencies which are not on the preferred supplier list and is not responsible for any related fees for resumes submitted to job postings, our employees, or any other part of our company.

Job Details
• Financial Planning & Analysis: Input and manage financial data related to Plan, Estimate, and Long Range Plan (LRP) into our Enterprise Planning Management (EPM) system.
• Monthly Reporting: assist in preparation of monthly reporting packages and other business presentations as required; prepare ad hoc reports as needed.
• Analysis & Modeling: Evaluate, estimate, and analyze financial and operational impacts of new business prospects.
• Variance Reporting: Identify and report on variances/changes by reconciling data within various financial systems and data warehouse applications.
• Provide in-depth analysis of deviations.
• Expense Management: Oversee operating expenses for departments including operations, distribution, and commercial organizations that drive key distribution channels.
• Analytical Reporting: Generate year-over-year, quarter-over-quarter, and month-to-date analysis comparing actual results against Plan/Estimate/Prior year metrics for key expense accounts and business drivers.
• Collaboration With Finance Team: Partner with the Finance team to ensure timely updates to estimates and provide monthly financial reporting to senior management.

.

Qualifications:
• Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting or related field; CPA or CFA certification is a plus.
• Minimum of 3 years’ experience in financial planning & analysis within a large corporation or global business service environment.
• Proficiency in Enterprise Planning Management (EPM) systems and Workday.
• Strong analytical skills with advanced proficiency in Excel; experience with data visualization tools is advantageous.
• Excellent communication skills with demonstrated ability to work collaboratively across different teams.

Thornton Tomasetti applies engineering and scientific principles to solve the world’s challenges. An independent organization of creative thinkers and innovative doers collaborating from offices worldwide, our mission is to bring our clients' ideas to life and, in the process, lay the groundwork for a better, more resilient future. We provide support and opportunities to our employees to achieve their full potential and cultivate a rewarding career.

The Role

We are seeking a highly experienced and analytical Senior Financial Analyst with a strong background in data-driven decision-making to join our team. This role is ideal for a business major with 8–10 years of experience who is fluent in online analysis tools including Excel (advanced), Power BI, Power Query, and other data visualization and modeling platforms.

As a strategic partner to cross-functional teams, you will leverage your technical and business expertise to build and maintain venues which drive firm-wide performance, deliver automated reporting, and yield enable actionable insights.

Responsibilities:

•Data Analysis & Reporting:

Collect, clean, and analyze large data sets from various sources to identify trends, anomalies, and business opportunities.

•Dashboard Development:

Build and maintain interactive dashboards and visual reports using Power BI, MS Dynamics and related tools, and Excel, enabling leadership to make data-driven decisions.

•Performance Tracking:

Develop KPIs and performance metrics to monitor key business activities and improve operational efficiency.

•Stakeholder Collaboration:

Work closely with teams across finance, marketing, operations, and IT to understand data needs and translate business questions into analytical solutions.

•Process Automation:

Streamline and automate manual reporting processes using tools like Power Query, VBA, and macros in Excel.

Requirements:

•Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance and/or Business Systems.

•8–10 years of experience

•Fluent w/supporting experience in online analysis tools including Excel (advanced), Power BI, Power Query, and other data visualization and modeling platforms.

•Demonstrated ability to consider & engage AI tools in the data management and business analytics space.

•Proven ability to work develop/train less experienced staff in said areas.

•Excellent organizational skills.

DESCRIPTION

Responsible for the preparation and analysis of financial and statutory reports at Legal Entity and Department level. Supports corporate functions, regulatory compliance requirements, and business stakeholders through financial reporting, budgeting, forecasting, and performance analysis. Contributes to regulatory reporting including BRSR, RPT, MSME, listed entity compliances, and other evolving statutory requirements within the India region. Ensures integrity of financial data, supports audit processes, and drives continuous improvement in compliance and reporting processes.

Key Responsibilities Financial Reporting & Analysis
• Prepare and analyze financial reports for corporate functions and department managers.
• Compile and present legal entity-level analysis for board presentations.
• Prepare statistical reporting including financial performance analysis, variance explanations, and cost analysis.
• Interpret financial data by analyzing trends, risks, and opportunities to provide insights to stakeholders.
• Evaluate financial results against targets and communicate conclusions effectively.
• Support month-end close activities including data preparation and consolidation.

Budgeting & Forecasting
• Partner with assigned organizational units to develop annual budgets.
• Support expense and staffing forecasts.
• Assist in statistical and financial forecasting processes to align with business objectives.

Regulatory & Compliance Reporting
• Support regulatory reporting requirements including BRSR, RPT, MSME, and listed entity compliances.
• Lead BRSR reporting at the legal entity level, particularly Social and Governance parameters.
• Manage statutory audit of core BRSR parameters and coordinate reporting on value chain partners.
• Execute special projects to ensure compliance with the evolving regulatory landscape.
• Streamline compliance processes across legal entities within the India region.
• Maintain compliance calendar and ensure timely filings and disclosures.
• Monitor cross-functional stakeholder inputs and support frontend audit activities and reporting to authorities.

Business Partnering & Continuous Improvement
• Collaborate with stakeholders to drive performance aligned with organizational goals and strategies.
• Influence decision-making through strong financial insight and business acumen.
• Identify opportunities to optimize work processes and improve reporting efficiency.
• Support special projects and continuous improvement initiatives as assigned.

RESPONSIBILITIES

Skills & Competencies

Technical Skills
• Financial Analysis – Ability to analyze trends, variances, risks, and opportunities to drive business improvement.
• Financial Reporting – Preparation and presentation of financial information in compliance with regulations and stakeholder expectations.
• Budgeting & Forecasting – Supporting annual planning cycles and expense/staffing forecasts.
• US GAAP Knowledge – Understanding and application of US accounting standards and company policies.
• Regulatory Reporting – Knowledge of BRSR, RPT, MSME, listed entity compliances, and statutory reporting requirements (preferred).

Behavioral Competencies
• Action Oriented – Takes on new challenges with urgency and enthusiasm.
• Collaborates – Builds strong partnerships to achieve shared objectives.
• Communicates Effectively – Delivers clear and concise communication tailored to different audiences.
• Demonstrates Self-Awareness – Seeks and applies feedback for growth.
• Nimble Learning – Learns through experimentation and adapts quickly.
• Optimizes Work Processes – Drives efficiency and continuous improvement.
• Self-Development – Actively pursues learning and professional growth.
• Values Differences – Embraces diverse perspectives and cultures.
• Business Partnering – Influences stakeholders and aligns financial insights with business strategy.

QUALIFICATIONS

Qualifications

Education
• College, university, or equivalent degree in Finance, Accounting, or related field required.
• CA qualification or progress toward a professional certification is preferred.

Experience
• Minimal to some relevant professional work experience in Finance or Accounting required.
• Intermediate level knowledge obtained through training or on-the-job experience.
• Exposure to financial reporting, budgeting, forecasting, or regulatory compliance is preferred.
• Experience supporting audits or working with statutory reporting frameworks is advantageous.

Job Description

About Citco:

The market leader. The premier provider. The best in the business. At Citco, we've been the front-runner in our field since our incorporation in 1948 led to the evolution of the asset servicing sector itself. This pioneering spirit continues to guide us today as we innovate and expand, push beyond the boundaries of our industry, and shape its future. From working exclusively with hedge funds to serving all alternatives, corporations and private clients, our organization has grown immensely across asset classes and geographies. For us, this progress is a pattern that we'll only maintain as we move forward, always prioritizing our performance. So for those who want to play at the top of their game and be at the vanguard of their space, we say: Welcome to Citco.

About the Team & Business Line:

Fund Administration is Citco's core business, and our alternative asset and accounting service is one of the industry's most respected. Our continuous investment in learning and technology solutions means our people are equipped to deliver a seamless client experience.

Responsibilities

Your Role:
• You will produce accurate custom and regulatory risk management and fund performance analytics reports to be distributed to hedge fund clients, their investors and regulatory bodies in a timely manner
• Resolve all queries to risk reports
• Support the new business process - on-boarding new clients, assisting in the preparation of demos, marketing literature, maintaining demo risk system and product development (eg exploring/researching/bringing to market possible new revenue streams such as in response to emerging regulations)
• Be involved in the maintenance, prototyping and user acceptance testing of internally developed valuation models and risk tools
• Perform operational risk management - risk reporting process documentation, improving processes through increasing level of automation, ensure consistent application of CFS policies and procedures, identify and appropriately communicate potential internal and external risks.
• Assist relationship managers by participating in monthly calls or any escalation relating to day-to-day risk reporting issues, participate in communication/escalation aspects of complex issues resolution
• Contribute to cross functional training initiatives

Qualifications

About You:
• You have a quantitative background with a Bachelor/higher level degree or professional qualification (MSc, PhD, CQF, FRM, PRMIA, GARP, CFA, FIA)
• A quick learner who is self-motivated and demonstrates a strong attention to detail while multi-tasking
• Excellent oral and written communication skills and interpersonal skills
• Proficient in Excel, VBA, SQL and Python
• 0-2 years of experience in Financial Services, preferably with detailed knowledge of pricing/valuing/risk management of OTC derivatives using both in-house models/financial libraries/risk systems and specialist vendors such as Bloomberg BVAL, SuperDerivatives and IHS Markit
• Knowledge of investment risk measurement and management under regulatory frameworks such as Form PF, Form CPO-PQR, Annex IV, Basel III/CRD IV/CRR and Solvency II is advantageous

Our Benefits

Your well-being is of paramount importance to us, and central to our success. We provide a range of benefits, training and education support, and flexible working arrangements to help you achieve success in your career while balancing personal needs. Ask us about specific benefits in your location.

We embrace diversity, prioritizing the hiring of people from diverse backgrounds. Our inclusive culture is a source of pride and strength, fostering innovation and mutual respect.

Citco welcomes and encourages applications from people with disabilities. Accommodations are available upon request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection.

Nasdaq Technology is looking for a passionate QA Test Specialist with a focus on software testing, to join the Mumbai technology center in India. If Innovation and effectiveness drive you forward this is the place for you! Nasdaq is continuously revolutionizing markets and undergoing transformations while we adopt new technologies to develop innovative solutions, constantly aiming to rewrite tomorrow. As a QA Test Specialist, you will play a key role in enhancing the quality of our platform delivery through various test methodologies. We are seeking a highly motivated and innovative individual to join our Mumbai/Pune based Quality Assurance team. The position oversees the Automation testing of Market Risk and Regulatory Risk in Calypso Trading Solution. This is a tremendous opportunity to strengthen technical, business & product skills.We are looking for candidates who share those values along with a genuine desire and drive to deliver top technology solutions to today's markets.You will collaborate with cross-functional teams across the globe, ensuring the highest standards of quality in solutions and services delivered to world-leading Capital Market Participants. In this role, you will have the opportunity to work in a strong authority team delivering complex technology solutions to international clients. You will work with and learn from subject matter authorities, and have the opportunity to seek more responsibility and progress within the Quality Assurance team. Besides working closely with your colleagues in Mumbai, you will also work closely with Nasdaq teams in other countries.Furthermore, you will: Guarantee the quality and the persistence of the software by understanding the requirement/specification document and crafting test cases to collect bugs within the system. Work in close collaboration with both our Product Management and Engineering teams to ensure inclusion of improvements requested by clients in the software and ensure delivery. Develop, develope, & execute manual and automated test solutions. Investigate test failures in automated tests.Detailing test cases and maintain the test library.At least 2-4 years of experience in Capital Market, preferably Market Risk/Regulatory Risk within Product/Software company or Investment BankGood Experience required in analyzing portfolios with different risk factors (credit risk, liquidity risk, market risk, and operational risk)In-depth understanding of various risk reports such as Value-at-Risk (VaR) and Historical Simulation (HS), and the risk modeling techniques that underlie such reportsGood Experience required in completing scenario tests and stress tests for financial risk models.Experience in Trade Capture for any of the asset classAbility to read complex requirement/specification document and design test cases.Strong ability to work independently in a techno functional environmentEducation Qualification: Bachelor/Master in Engineering, Mathematics, Finance, Business, Economics.It would be great if youKnowledge on any of the asset class (EQ, FI, COM, FX, FXO, IRD, CDS, MM, Derivatives, Pricing, Repo).Knowledge of Software Testing Life CycleFair understanding of Database and SQLKnowledge of Xray or Quality CenterCome as You AreNasdaq is an equal opportunity employer.
